description_long: This street food stall consists of a compact, rectangular metal structure with a flat roof, set on a tiled red platform. A tall street lamp with overhead wires stands adjacent to the stall, providing illumination and urban context. The stall features a serving counter at the front, with a large sign displaying menu items and prices, and various cooking utensils and containers are visible on and around the counter. A seated vendor is present beside the stall, adding a human element and suggesting active service. The setup includes a bucket, a bowl with utensils, and several food preparation items, indicating a functional and busy food service environment. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with exposed metal surfaces and practical arrangements for quick street-side food preparation and service.
description: A compact metal street food stall with a flat roof, serving counter, signage, and adjacent street lamp, featuring a seated vendor and various cooking utensils.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the stall from an angled perspective, highlighting the metal roof, the street lamp, and a bowl with utensils on the tiled ground.
description_view_1: View 1 presents the front of the stall, displaying the large menu sign, serving counter, and the vendor seated beside the structure.
description_view_2: View 2 captures the rear of the stall, focusing on the back counter, the vendor, and the street lamp with visible overhead wires.
description_view_3: View 3 provides a top-down side view, emphasizing the roof, the arrangement of cooking equipment, and the vendor partially obscured by the lamp post.
attribute_signage: A large rectangular sign with yellow and red text and images is attached to the front of the stall, displaying menu items and prices in a bold, readable style.
attribute_serving_counter: The serving counter is a flat, rectangular metal surface at the front of the stall, wide enough for food preparation and customer interaction, with a menu sign attached below.
attribute_food_display: Food items and utensils are displayed openly on the counter and in containers, easily accessible to the vendor and visible to customers, with no protective glass or cover.
